The receipts are for a two-year accounting, so it appears that annual income should be half the amount shown in paragraph 8. If time was billed by a paralegal, the attorney must address his/her qualifications regarding the requirements of, and compliance with, Business & Professions Code Section 6450. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DHIREN DWIJEN DESAI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. APPEARANCE FOR NO RESPONSE: If a hearing is continued for issues raised by an examiner to be addressed and there is no response prior to the continued hearing date, the matter is automatically continued again, usually for one month. If there is still no response, an appearance is required. If no one appears after the automatic second continuance, the matter is ordinarily taken off calendar if it is not a compulsory petition, or an Order to Appear is issued. Numerous issues with this petition for final distribution were posted for the hearing on 7/11/23. Nothing has been filed to address them. The Court may consider sanctions, suspension, or removal. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN EDWARD DURHAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues (see tentative rulings for the previous 3 hearings) remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 08/23/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petition must be verified by the administrator. It should also be noted that no inventory and appraisal has been filed since letters were issued to the administrator in 05/16/2022. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS S. CHURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/13/23. (No earlier availability.) Issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 7/11/23 have not been addressed or sufficiently addressed. 1. The petitioner's verified supplement to her second amended petition has not yet been filed. 2. For some reason, the proof of service filed on 9/14/23 shows that a NOTICE OF HEARING (Judicial Council form DE-120) was used again to give notice of the second amended petition, where the ONLY correct form of notice when someone is petitioning to open an estate is the NOTICE OF [Second Amended] PETITION TO ADMINISTER ESTATE (Judicial Council form DE-121). Deadline for addressing these issues is 12/4/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIAN YATES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the copy of the supplement received by the examiner was not a true and correct copy of the document (most of pp 3 and 4 were cut off), and a supplement (or amendment) to a petition should stand on its own and not be an "answer" to issues noted by an examiner. NB: When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY ANNE MARTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/29/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. "Petitioner" is not relationship to the decedent. A person is either related to the decedent or is a stranger. 2. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Whose children are the niece and nephews? Deadline is 11/20/23 for providing the courtesy copy to the examiner of a supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WANDA MAY LOO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/14/23. The following issues need to be cleared two weeks prior: (1) Petition #8 to identify the trustee of the devisee trust. (2) Petition #8 to state David Loo (husband)'s info, including the date of death. (3) Also, if the proposed executor is the trustee, all beneficiaries under the terms of the trust must be identified and noticed. (4) Allegation if the (San Mateo County) PG's Office has power to receive Elaine Loo's mails. CRC 7.51(a)(2). Otherwise, direct mailing to Elaine Loo is required. (5) Need to attach a copy of the will to Petition. Prob. Code sec. 8002(b)(1). Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
